My only substitution was garbanzo beans for black beans, I went light on the cheese, and I only had salted tortilla chips (they tasted fine). I will make this again and again again! As good as any chaat I have had!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Cook boneless chicken thighs in masala sauce and shred if you want a meat addition.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Brilliant recipe! Only recommend mixing the Greek yoghurt with some black salt, chaat and cumin powders and loosening it up with some water before sprinkling on top. Oh! Those are common Indian spices with a little tang and super delicious! One packet could last for months..

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We make this regularly (albeit with less cheese and store-bought tamarind chutney) as it’s a hit with kids. Should you end up with leftovers, toast them in a hot skillet and top with an egg and any leftover sauces. They’ll never be *crisp* again, but they will be delicious.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Maybe the best thing I’ve ever tasted. No substitutions, no amendments.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: So delicious.  But make sure to oil the foil.  My nachos were completely stuck to the foil.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I make my Indian nachos with next day rajma and then add the frills as wanted - mix and match salsas, cheeses etc. yum!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Good tamarind sauce involves blending dates and cooking them with tamarind pulp and water, plus some chili pepper.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Wow!
These are truly an exceptional twist on nachos!
As other suggested, substituted Pomegranate Molasses for Tamarind. 
These are going into the rotation!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Pomegranate molasses with maple syrup and lime was yummy.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Recipe looks delicious aside from aversion to Cilantro. Suggestions for substitutions.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Use pomegranate molasses for tamarind sauce.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I don't leave comments on recipes but I had to remark on how absolutely delicious this one is. Quick and easy for a weeknight dinner. We have made this a few times now, just as is, and we will make it again and again and again. Don't skip on the greek yogurt. Delicious!!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Daaaaang these are good! Like soooo good.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Had to make this last night. The roma tomatoes are piling up, tomato pie Thursday, nachos on Friday. Doubled the tomatoes. Mixed the beans with canned chopped mild green chiles.

Had no cilantro in the house. Made the chhonk with minced fresh jalapeno, 1/4 tsp cayenne, 1 tsp cumin powder, oil and lime juice. Added pink salt and cracked pepper.

Used blue chips, sweet onion. Needed green on top so we dolloped SWAD Green Chili Chutney and drizzled the chhonk. I had sour cream with my serving(s).

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Unbelievably good. I used pickled red onions instead of raw and cut the cheese by half (and they were still super cheesy!). Devoured by all at the table, including a toddler.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I think it would be helpful to have A- proper measurements and B- some direction how to make tamarind sauce. I have tamarind concentrate and was struggling to find a recipe last minute to make a sauce. All in all, very tasty. I made sure not to eat too much based on other people’s suggestions.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Serving size says 6-8 but my household of 2 adults can easily polish this off in one sitting it’s SOOO good.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Reduced the cheese and Chhonk by half because I wasn't making this for a large group and didn't want all the fat. Couldn't find tamarind sauce, but I did find tamarind stir-fry paste and cut it with maple syrup and some lime juice for a sweet and sour flavor. If I were making this to entertain I'd consider topping it with shredded Butter Chicken. Flavor profile was on point. This would make a great party dish.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were a terrific new take on the same old nachos we all love, but can’t get excited to serve. The complexity of flavors is inspired and married well together. I made the recipe as written, including the addition of tamarind, however I did not use the full 1/4c.Instead, I drizzled thin zigzags across the nachos which made a nice presentation. For my taste, 1/4c of tamarind would have overpowered the dish, but that is personal preference. Husband was skeptical, but won over in first bite.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Our household is cilantro-averse, but we have loads of fresh parsley growing in our garden, and I think that would work just as well.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: My changes: beans are not my friend, so I cooked skinless, boneless chicken thighs in a crock pot with a masala simmer sauce from Trader Joe’s until it easy easily shredded. I had no time to go get tamarind sauce, so made a combo of the maple syrup & some sweet chutney. No cumin seeds, so I used ground cumin in ghee. I look forward to making it with tamarind sauce, but this is definitely a keeper

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This look delicious but I am not a fan of cilantro.  Any suggestions for a substitute?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Basil pesto. 
You could also try using basil and/or parsley instead of the cilantro in the chutney.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Mint is a common chutney ingredient instead of cilantro/coriander in Indian recipes. Basil is not used in Indian cuisine, so I'd use mint instead

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Truly outstanding! Key is to use thick tortilla chips.  I ran out of those so I added the thinner Xochitl chips, and those turned soggy under the weight of the toppings.   Will be making these for crowds often.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
This was an instant hit with the family. Some minor enhancements
1. Add cumin to the beans
2. Mix the cilantro chutney with the maple syrup chutney

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I may or may not have halved this recipe and made it...for myself. It was absolutely DELICIOUS and I have no regrets.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Seriously, who made this in 30 minutes?!? It’s about time the cooking times weren’t estimated by professionals! I am aging quickly, moving slower, but it took way too long.....
Having said that it was good, but soggy. Really should use thick chips, preferably from an authentic Mexican supplier. As for the Chhonk, I wasn’t that impressed, seemed to add just a lot of grease while toasted cumin seeds wouldn’t.
Would be a good brunch addition with fried, scrambled or well poached eggs on top.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Can’t stop making these nachos- what a wonderful array of flavors! I usually make a little extra cilantro chutney to top on soups or tacos. And I tend to go a little lighter on the oil for the chhonk, so that it’s just a helpful lubricant for the cumin seeds

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Wow! I do a lot of cooking and this might be the best thing I have cooked all year. After serving at a party there was not one crumb left!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: One of the best recipes I’ve EVER tried — dangerous because I crave it all the time now!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I make the tamarind sauce with store bought tamarind pulp (has pits/seeds and not very smooth) and treat it as such: take small meatball size of tamarind pulp and a little more than 1/4 cup water and microwave in bowl for 1 min, mix together with fork. Microwave a little more to fully combine and whisk again. Strain thru mesh sieve and voilà! A little work but very worth it

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are great! And balanced! And fun! I have made them twice. For me 1/4 cup of ghee is too much, made them a touch greasy so the second time, I used just enough to toast the cumin seeds.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: One of the best dishes I’ve ever made. Weird to say that about nachos, but it checks all the flavor and texture boxes. It’s fun to make as an appetizer when you have company over, except you may never make it to dinner as no one want to pull themselves away until the sheet pan is empty.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were great nachos!  I would have liked more of the chutney and less of the chhonk - otherwise, they were perfect!  Maple syrup was a suitable substitute for the tamarind sauce.  It cut the spice of the chutney and really made the dish come together.  Definitely make again!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I have been dipping my homemade cheese and chicken quesadillas in jarred coriander chutney (Indian market) for years! Never going back to salsa!!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: So delicious! I cooked up the black beans with some onion and green bell pepper to add some more warmth and richness. Thanks to the poster for the tamarind sauce recipe.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was so delicious! Definitely takes nachos to a new level. The moment when you pull the bubbly nachos out of the oven and add the chutney, chhonk, and tamarind sauce is the best. We added jalapenos because we love our nachos spicy, and they were a great addition. I will definitely be making this again!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I substituted homemade green tomato chutney for the tamarind sauce.  Delicious, this recipe is a winner.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Added about a teaspoon of sugar to 1/4 concentrated cooking tamarind to get the sweetness I'm assuming you get with the tamarind sauce. Loved this recipe!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made it as directed tonight.  A fair bit of work and clean up for Nachos. I liked the complex tastes that came through, but my wife was put off by the chhonk (I used the maple syrup and lime juice combo).  Not sure I would make again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Great flavour sensation! I only add more tomatoes, beans and onion to give it a more substantial filling.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Never made nachos until this and wow, it was delicious. Used date syrup and lime as had no tamarind paste (long gone from my fridge). Until the moment of eating wasn't sure how it would turn out. Five of us finished it right off.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Enjoyed these very much, although I wish I had made more cilantro chutney. I also added some paneer, cut into small cubes and seared in turmeric and pepper: they lent quite a meaty vibe to the dish.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Definitely needed a lot more lime juice that what was recommended for the cilantro chutney. We didn't use any tamarind chutney as we didn't want a sweet flavor. A really enjoyable outcome!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are CRAZY GOOD! We had them for dinner last night. We used the maple syrup/lime juice sub for tamarind sauce. Also served the nachos on a bed of slightly crunchy loose leaf lettuce. I found myself wanting to come up with more recipes using these flavors.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: LOVED! 2 people devoured a dish meant for 6-8. That's a powerful love.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Husband now prefers these nachos over any other. Keeper.....the recipe too.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Aha! I found a recipe for Vegan “queso” cheese elsewhere on NYT, so that can be used here.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious -- but DO NOT line the pan with foil!  I'm not sure why I followed this direction -- you end up peeling tiny bits of foil off of melted cheese and therefore losing part of the dish & wasting a lot of time. I'll try parchment paper next time, or nothing at all.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Alternative to cilantro?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are awesome - and hearty... they fed four people for dinner.  I do have a suggestion for a hack:  next time you get Indian take-out, save the sauces.  Make the chips & veggies portion of the recipe, and then drizzle on the tamarind sauce and cilantro chutney that came with the takeout.  But do make the chhonk!  It gilds the lily.  And have lots of Greek yogurt for dipping.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Pomegranate molasses made a delicious substitution for the tamarind sauce. The whole family loved this!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made these yesterday and actually messed up and made the tamarind sauce with 1/4 C lemongrass turmeric paste, 1/4 C maple syrup, juice of a half of lime, and a pinch of salt. So good - wish I had doubled it. Also used all the beans on the first layer of chips and then shredded rotisserie chicken on the top layer.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I was skeptical with the sauces and chhonk but made as directed and what a hit! We made it our dinner and found ourselves spooning even more chutney onto our plates. The chhonk makes the dish. Definitely use parchment paper instead of foil to line the pan as another cook suggested. 
Cannot wait to make this for company to have with cocktails. I’m borrowing the cookbook from the library today!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! A few notes for next time: 1) We felt it needed more beans. 2) Next time I'll put some of the chutneys on the bottom layer before baking, 3) Next time I'll make a raita instead of plain Greek yogurt. Definitely a hit!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I love these! 
I can’t believe how much salt the cilantro chutney calls for- I added half and thought it was too salty. My only other complaint would be that it doesn’t provide a tamarind sauce recipe- but there are plenty online if you can find tamarind paste.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
2 tbsp  seedless Tamarind, soaked 
1½ cup Water
1 tsp red chili powder or paprika
1/2 tsp coriander powder
1/4 tsp cumin powder
4 tbsp Jaggery 
6-8 Dates, seedless 
Salt to taste 


For Tamarind Chutney
In a pan add strained tamarind pulp, water, , jaggery, dates, salt, and boil it for 15-20 minutes on medium heat.
Once boiled, grind it properly using a hand blender and add chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder . Mix well .let it cool down completely. Store  it in the fridge .

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was a fun variation of the standard nachos - served them at a party and they were a success.  I may add a little more of the cilantro chutney and tamarind sauce next time.  For the tamarind sauce, I stirred 1 Tbsp tamarind concentrate into 2 Tbsp boiling water, then added 1 Tbsp maple syrup.  As recommended by some, I used the multi-grain scoop chips and they held up well.  Nice to be able to prep the sauces and the rest of the ingredients ahead of time.  Thanks for the recipe!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I absolutely love this, and have made it a few times for parties. Everyone loves the twist (ghee, yogurt, cumin..).  It is a keeper.  I just use a store-bought date-tamarind sauce.  Definitely a keeper.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I was unable to find tamarind sauce so winged it a bit by mixing maple syrup, fresh lime juice, some fig jam, and a little chaat masala. The fig jam idea came from a recipe in Priya’s book. It was tasty, and I’d do it again in a pinch.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
WOW these are truly the best nachos ever, the chhonk really makes it. 

I made tamarind sauce using 2 tbsp tamarind paste, 1 tbsp sambal, 1 crushed garlic clove, 1 tsp sugar, a big pinch of salt and chaat masala, and 1/4 cup hot water. And I didn't have enough cilantro for the chutney so I used half cilantro and half mint, which was delicious!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious but next time I will bake on a single layer on sheet pans - the bottom layer was soggy.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are AMAZING!!!  Made exactly as recipe states.  These will be my go-to party trick nachos.  I found tamarind sauce somewhere - the Fresh Market maybe?  I will be adding it to dahl and curries or roast chicken/potatoes.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Dr. Strangelove: or how I learned to stop worrying and eat a full pound of cheese and a stick of butter over nachos with just one other person.

Stupidly involved for nachos and worth every bit of it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was SO good and flavorful! We substituted kidney beans for black beans and seasoned them with some chili to add more of a kick, The only complaint was there was far too little beans in ratio with everything else. Next time I'll be doubling or even tripling the beans for this dish, but I'll definitely be making it again!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Honestly one of the best things I’ve ever eaten. Makes a ton of nachos and would be great for a party.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: A pound of cheese? ...not sure I can do that, maybe I'll try half. Americanizing something doesn't have to mean covering something with cheese.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: What part of "Indian-ish" has convinced you it's Americanized? Cheese is made all over the world. Fondue uses quite a bit of cheese. It's Swiss.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We really liked this.  My only warning is the huge amount of salt in the cilantro chutney. Reduce it along with the amount of cheese,  I used half the amount and it was plenty.  Otherwise, delicious!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Massive hit with the fam. Substituted vegan cheese and dairy free yogurt. Delicious! fYI - violife shredded cheese has a great melt dynamic and tastes good too

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Perfection! Followed the recipe. Did the maple syrup/lime option. Everyone was blown away by the flavors. My husband gave me Indian-ish for Christmas because I like Priya so much so I’m stoked to see her getting well deserved love for this recipe.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made these for a family get together - they were a HUGE hit. Made as specified except no onion.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely outstanding. I thought the cilantro chutney was going to be too spicy because I added a really hot pepper and when I tasted it I thought oh *#*#Not even sure what it was but it was green thin pepper in an unmarked bin at whole foods. But when it was mixed with the tamarind and toppings it wasn't over powering at all. I'm excited to make this for everyone I know. For now my husband and I polished off the entire sheet pan and I'm not sorry haha

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Truly different, or as my husband said odd. It did not keep us from eating it. There was just a lot of cognitive dissonance Nachos vs indian food. We followed the directions as written and enjoyed the tamarind sauce and the cilantro chutney contrasts. As another reviewer said this makes a lot, you really need 6 - 8 people to serve it to.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Sauteed red onion, then added shredded, grilled chicken breast, garam masala and water to moisten. Two layers of: low-sodium tortilla chips, sharp cheddar, chicken mixture, chopped tomatoes, red onion, black beans, corn kernels. Baked @ 375 for 20 min. Served with the cilantro chutney (take it very easy on the lime), homemade tamarind chutney and Greek yogurt. Absolutely amazing.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Please DO NOT use maple syrup and lime juice for tamarind chutney substitute! It would be like pouring maple syrup over your nachos. Too strong maple taste and not right consistency     The lengths I went to salvage mine was way more involved.   My tamarind paste wasn't delivered in time so I had to improvise. I've found best flavors to come from pureed prunes(thanks to having toddlers) & dates. Play around with additional spices, salt, ingredients to get the right flavor.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: The nachos were tasty, but that amount of ghee was overkill and we all felt a bit queasy after because it was so rich. Next time I would reduce it and also the sweet sauce, to keep it a bit more savory.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Subbed the beans with tandoori chicken (marinated overnight and broiled in the oven). Would try the reco’s here for jazzing up the tamarind sauce which seemed a bit flat to me. Added way more red chili powder, closer to 1/4 teaspoon to give a bit more kick. Also, grate your own cheese for better melting vs. using pre-shredded cheese covered in anti-caking powder. All in all still a great recipe!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: The best advice here was to halve the recipe if only serving two.  We definitely would have eaten the whole thing and made ourselves sick.  Truly too good.  Can’t wait to serve for a gathering.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made as written, it was a huge hit for a family lunch. The only thing I will change next time is that I will not line the pan with foil. The cheese stuck to it and so we lost a fair bit of the nachos. As the recipe author says, do NOT skip the drizzle of "chhonk." It makes the dish!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely excellent. The flavors and elements come together perfectly.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was spectacular but needlessly high in calories. We used torn tortillas instead of tortilla chips, and 2T canola oil instead of butter for the chhonk. We added a second can of beans too for a heftier meal. Loved it and have made it twice so far.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic! These are my new favorite snack and so quick to throw together! I happened to pick up some zhoug from Trader Joe’s so I used that in place of the cilantro chutney. I made the tamarind sauce by mixing about 2 teaspoons of tamarind paste with a few tablespoons of date syrup. It was quite sweet but can be adjusted to taste with more tamarind or a little lime juice. I only used 8 ounces of cheese as I found a full pound to be unnecessary.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this multiple times. My preferred ratios are to use 1.5-2x the beans and cut back the cheese to no more than 12 ounces.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: My picky 18-month old loved these!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: So so so good, 10/10. Our alterations: kidney beans instead of black, maple syrup with lemon juice instead of tamarind and lime, and 2 cups of cheese instead of 4. We thought everything was just right - the spice from the cayenne with the sweetness of the maple syrup with the salt from the chips and chutney all paired together perfectly!!!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Terrific, versatile recipe. Coudn’t find tamarind sauce so I added corn to the nachos and used tomato achaar as one of the topping sauces.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We enjoyed this recipe but not nearly as much as other readers. ‘Good different’ or a novel but not a must have again. It had breadth but not depth of flavour. Our usual sauce is a mix of Tom purée with a table spoon of olive oil and 1-2 teaspoons of cumin powder (and chillie to taste) stirred thru it, we layer chips, sauce (dont get it soggy), juilenne capsicum and cheese, grilled, repeated all layers and serve with the usual sides. Yum!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
These nachos were really tasty, mostly (I think) because of the chutney. That cilantro chutney is delicious! I was happy to have a bit extra of it because I could eat it with everything else I cooked this week.

I used olive oil and ground cumin and cayenne for the chhonk, because that is what I had, and I'm not sure it added much to the nachos. I might try this again at some point with ghee and cumin seeds.

I plan to make this next time I host a gathering - good party dish.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Anyone try this with chickpeas instead of black beans?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. I made the suggested substitute for tamarind sauce - maple syrup with lime juice. It doesn’t really need the chhonk imo, altho I made it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I served this to a group of teenagers and it was a hit with them and my whole family. They were skeptical as I added the cilantro/pepper mixture, but no denying the mix of flavors was incredible. No substitutions.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Could I use tamarind paste or tamarind concentrate? What exactly is tamarind 'sauce'?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I like to use Maggi tamarind sauce for this recipe (which I love).  Any Indian grocery will have it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
upstairs woman is right for the sauce. but tamarind paste or concentrate is pure tamarind. sauce has added sweetener and some spices.
2 tbsp  seedless Tamarind, soaked 
1½ cup Water
1 tsp red chili powder or paprika
1/2 tsp coriander powder
1/4 tsp cumin powder
4 tbsp Jaggery 
6-8 Dates, seedless 
Salt to taste 


For Tamarind Chutney
In a pan add strained tamarind pulp, water, , jaggery, dates, salt, and boil it for 15-20 minutes on medium heat.
Once boiled, grind it properly using a hand blend

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made with instant-pot pinto beans (I was out of black) and the Maple/lime substitution which added just the right amount of sweetness and bite to all the salty goodness of the cheese. Truly addictive in the best way nachos can be!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made this using the Vegan Queso (cashew based) recipe also from NYT Cooking--it turned out really well! Tofutti sour cream on top. Tamarind cooking concentrate works well as a sauce.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
This is fabulous, and a very successful party dish. I kept to the recipe, only used a multi grain tortilla chip, which held up well. 
So happy to learn about chhonk and to see the NewYorker video. This recipe is a keeper.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: M Shah That's what I would do but you would have to add a sweetener either maple syrup, honey or sugar.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are hands down the best nachos I've made or eaten. I followed the recipe except didnt have tamarind. The chhonk is a revelation.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We all loved this! I forgot to put the tamarind sauce on so I'll be doing it again very soon.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is outstanding. Great flavor. Enjoyed with plain Greek yogurt. Forgot to season the black beans but they really didn’t need it. Used wraps (baked) for the plain chips. I will make this again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were a lovely taste treat & I made them strictly according to the recipe.  I agree with Sarah B & would cut down the amt of ghee - just need enough to deal w/ the 2T of cumin seeds.  They were a little soggy so would also cut down on the amt of cilantro chutney.  Tasty & different - will make again w/ these adjustments.  Also agree with K White & wd drizzle the tamarind sauce over the top (not use whole 1/4C)

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Did people find the greek yogurt to be a huge flavor addition or not necessary?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made these for a Super Bowl dinner last night and followed the directions to the letter. The reviews were consistent:  most amazing nachos ever.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were a hit for superbowl party and not too spicy for those who don't like hot pepper spice.  I had no tamarind, and used pomegranate molasses instead.  Would cook again for sure.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made this for Super Bowl Sunday (Congratulations, Chiefs and Andy Reid).  Cut it down a bit, but otherwise followed the recipe. Did not have cumin seeds, so just used cumin and had lime tortilla chips, which worked well. In future, would use olive oil for cilantro mixture, as I think it would hold together a bit better. Loved the taste of the tamarind sauce and will find many other ways to use it up. Was very tasty and filling and will probably try few things to mix it up next time.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These sound amazing and we love spicy Indian cuisine. However, one of our family members hates black beans (sadly). He does like chickpeas. For those who have made this, do you think they are a viable substitute for the black beans?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Way late on this response, but I think chickpeas would be perfect! They seem even more indian-friendly than black beans, although they might be prone to rolling off the chips :) I think any type of bean would work well, really!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This comes together so fast and it’s a terrific twist on nachos. My kids didn’t miss having meat. The flavors are perfectly balanced. The only thing I’ll do differently is use two to three peppers and more cayenne for a little heat. So good!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I'm not a huge fan of beans, but this turned out really well! We skipped the chhonk and I ended up substituting a mango chutney because the tamarind syrup that I ordered didn't arrive on time, but my husband loved it. I will probably chop the onion a bit more coarsely the next time, and increase the amount of cheddar cheese, but stick with the beans.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We made these on a Saturday in the fall, thinking nachos would be a perfect snack for watching the Michigan game. We were right about the nachos, but it turned out Michigan had a bye week, so we watched Rudy instead. We later brought these to Remy's for Halloween movie night.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Layers of great flavors. Delicious.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I would double the amount of beans though!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Could I use jalapeños for the green chiles?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Such a win! LOVED this. A beautiful balance of texture, color, and sweet/salty/tart bright flavors. No substitutions other than pinto beans for black beans; I really think any bean type would work just fine. I’ll definitely be making this again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!!! Used salsa instead of chopped tomato/onion and the maple syrup variation in place of tamarind sauce. Addictive combination of spicy, sweet, buttery sauces! These nachos are so good I bought Priya's Indian-ish cookbook

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Tremendous recipe! A delightful mix of flavors. My family could not get enough. The only ingredient I added was seasoned and cooked turkey. Didn’t need it! Will make this again soon.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! No big changes for me except I added garam masala to the beans instead of the chonk, also added a big drizzle of sour cream on top and put extra chutney on the side. So good!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Addicting!  The cilantro chutney, tamarind sauce, and Chhonk definitely take nachos to the next level. Making the cilantro chutney in a blender was tricky due to the initial lack of liquid, but I was amazed how 4 cups of cilantro blended to a small amount.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely adored this! The cilantro chutney was phenomenal and I will be making it again later this week as a marinade for shrimp - it is extremely versatile. I was not able to find tamarind sauce, but I did find whole tamarinds. So I soaked the tamarinds to get them soft enough to squeeze the meat off the seeds. I used about half tamarind pulp and half maple syrup/lime combo and it was great. I think the addition of the actual tamarind meat really added to the sauce and was worth the effort.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I found that I had almost none of the ingredients but I still wanted to make this, because nachos. And I thought my completely different mix still made good nachos.

Swapped Cilantro for half mint- half sweet basil. Ended up mixing this into the beef after cooking.
Browned ground beef and seasoned with mint/basil mix and tsp of garam masala. 
Made something like a tamarind sauce by using 2tsps of tamarind concentrate, 1tbsp of maple syrup, 1 tbsp of water. 
Used half queso fresco, half havarti.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Liked the basic idea. Did not care for the cilantro "chutney (too citrusy)," so would skip that and maybe scatter some cilantro over it . Loved the chhonk (used powder instead of seeds) and tamarind sauce, so would double those. All in all, made for a fun dinner, and would make again with modifications.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were super fantastic, i bought both the cilantro chutney and some tamarind sauce from our local Indian grocery and that elevated these nachos way up there.  Agree with others the Chhonk is mandatory.  We used the leftover Chhonk the next night on some roasted veggies.  Yum.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone subbed pomegranate syrup for the tamarind?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! I will definitely make again. I fed four adults using the quantities in the recipe, but it wouldn't have fed more unless people weren't hungry.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I did what one reviewer suggested and cooked chicken thighs in TJ Marsala simmer sauce in the instant pot for some additional protein.  I also picked up some tamarind/date syrup at a local Indian market (on the suggestion of the clerk) for those who can't find or don't want to make tamarind sauce.  Highly recommend the syrup as it added a nice sweet & sour note.  Also think I might add a bit more lime juice to the cilantro chutney next time too.  Outstanding recipe!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Chutney: 1C cilantro, 2 serranos, 1T lime, 1T water, regular amts. sugar and salt.

Nachos: add chicken using the NYT recipe for chicken tacos with adobo sauce. Didn't make the tamarind sauce...didn't miss it (but also didn't really have "Indian-ish nachos" in the end).

Chhonk: fun name! Messed up by just throwing it all together in a bowl rather than in a pan. Oops. Maybe next time.

Sour cream works if you skip all the Indian-ish stuff. The cilantro chutney tastes good in either cuisine.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: chhonk means tempering ..

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: So worth it!   These were delicious and hit all the satisfaction notes!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Really enjoyed this! Thank you!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These nachos are sooooo good. I recommend going light with the tamarind sauce, but otherwise, it’s perfection.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Could not find tamarind sauce, so used 2 T tamarind paste plus 2 T maple syrup.  Otherwise, made exactly as directed in recipe.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed and it was underwhelming. The tamarind sauce covers up a lot of the other flavors so I’d recommend reducing the amount drizzled on at the end. I’d also double the beans.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Hard to conceptualize this dairy free, still havent found an edible false cheese. Anybody try it with either cashew “cheese” or tofu? Or without cheese element at all? Looks so yummy otherwise.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This will be our election-watching snack. It's so delicious. I've been waiting for the perfect time to make again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We've made these three times. Outstanding every time. I followed the recipe precisely. The first, due to the pandemic, I was unable to find tamarind sauce and the maple syrup combo proved to be an excellent sub. It's true, the Chhonk is extraordinary and should not be skipped. The crunch is devine. I have served Greek yogurt with this dish all three times but no one needs it. A great dish for entertaining - fun, different and can all be prepared in advance.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Overall a very flavorful, satisfying batch of nachos with a twist. We made the recipe as written but added two serranos instead of one. I thought it was too rich and oily - no doubt from all the cheese and the oil chhonk topping. The 20-somethings in our household, however, loved it — even adding extra red pepper flakes to spice it up a bit. Next time I would add less cheese - as suggested by another poster too.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Wow these were good and the whole family as impressed!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Incredible! I thought it was going to be pretty good, but it exceeded my expectations. Made exactly as is but doubled the sauces (all three were so good together, the tamarind was definitely worth it if you can find it), but I can see modifying this in tons of delicious ways, just try to keep as many of the sauces as you can (including Greek yogurt for dipping)! Effort and time (low) to flavor payout (high) means I will absolutely make again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Karen - I think this could be made vegan and taste good with one of the cheese substitutes, I was thinking that this is one of those few recipes that a coconutty cheese sub (like daiya) could actually work with the flavors (I marinated my onions in coconut vinegar so it had a light coconut flavor). It just needs something melty or moist to bind the toppings to the chips, there is SO much flavor that the cheese is more practical than flavorful.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was a huge hit! We are mostly veggie so will 1.5 or double up on the beans next time. I found all other elements to be perfect in their proportions: no extra sauces to be abandoned in the fridge. Another reviewer mentioned greasy cheese: I used half the recommended cheese and we were happy with it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were great! I added yogurt to the cilantro chutney because my food processor wasn't mixing it well and it actually is turned out to be a great addition!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were delicious just following the recipe, and our toddler also approved! One thing, this is closer to an hour recipe.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made this, very similar to a dish I eat in Lahore, Pakistan. Next time will leave out the cheese which makes the dish too heavy. You can buy date-tamarind shop for cheap at (South-) Asian stores.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Wow. I will be adding Chhonk to all nachos from now until eternity. So good. I added some chopped up broccoli just to add another vegetable to it. This was great, and came together quickly. I’ll be adding this as a weeknight go-to!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I was expecting this to be a let down. It’s delicious!! I used store bought date syrup and it worked perfectly as a substitute for the tamarind.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is a definite “keeper”. Everyone loved it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I added a little lime achar with the tomatoes and onions before baking. It took the nachos to another level.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Served this on 2/4/2021 to the whole family. Made the recipe exactly as written. Everyone agreed that these were the best nachos we’ve ever had!  So full of flavor—savory, salty, sweet, a little spice...it had everything. Thank you for putting this out there!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was great and very flavorful! The chhonk with ghee added a really nice dimension. I used my go-to apple butter dupe for tamarind sauce (1/2c. apple butter, 1 tsp. cumin powder, 1 tsp. coriander powder, 1 tsp. red chili powder, 6 tbsp water and salt to taste) and it worked great.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, BUT, don't be like me and make an entire plate of nachos for yourself and then realize you have no one to share them with before they get soggy.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Foodie daughter described this as 'best nachos I have ever had in my life'.
My son loves beans - going to try it again, but using refried beans as a dip (or topping) which will be decided once I see their consistency. Hated that the black beans kept rolling off (and yes, I unsuccessfully 'slightly' mashed them as suggested).

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious. We had them for dinner, and I will definitely make this for parties. So tasty and pretty easy, too.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: What about substituting canned black eyed peas for the black beans? More similar in shape than chick peas but also Indian-ish.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Chickpeas can work but only if you boil them with black tea and baking soda after they are soaked for 8-10 hours.. the combination of two makes chickpeas soft to the core and keeps them juicy instead of dry even after they are boiled. it will be easy to press them slightly so that they don't roll over..

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Best nachos I have ever had. We did a family pub night and my teenage kids gobbled them up!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I cannot find tamarind paste anywhere.  Any help out there?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: savory spice shop has tamarind paste concentrate

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We’ve must have made this at least ten times by now. It’s so so good and will be family favorite for a long time, I predict!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was a delicious recipe and a real crowd pleaser.  It also prompted me to get the cookbook Indianish.  I had tamarind concentrate on hand which I diluted with water to a runny consistency and that worked fine.  A perfect party dish.  Thank you Priya and Ritu.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Very good, will make again

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Yo it was good, but so many dishes, especially for serving all the little sauces and toppings in. I used half the cheese recommended and it didn't suffer, but then again we aren't huge on melted cheese. These will be — WILL BE! — amazing for a potluck.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is amazing. I made it as an appetizer and my friends couldn't stop eating it -- we ended up finishing the plate and moving straight to dessert.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is only a review of the cilantro chutney at the top of the recipe. DELICIOUS and the work of seconds. I mixed it into plain yogurt to dip vegetables, and have enough left over for other applications to which I am eagerly looking forward.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Double the beans and half the cheese is our preference

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Tasty! Some chips got soggy cause I used a cast iron instead of sheet pan since I was reducing the recipe for 1 person. Stacked too tall. Lesson learned. Didn’t have tamarind so used the maple syrup/lime substitute and amazed that it worked and cut the maple out to achieve a mysterious sweet and tangy syrup.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is an amazing fusion of flavors! I doubled the beans, as we have it as a main dish. I didn't have enough cheddar cheese, so I combined it with some shredded jack. Silly me...I just used the tamarind paste straight, but we all loved the tartness of it. Definitely make the chhonk: It ties everything together. Next time I'll use a good commercial cilantro chutney...I couldn't get it smooth in the food processor, and I don't own a blender. It tasted fine...was just a little chunky!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Oh boy, this is remarkable — the whole family agreed best nachos we’ve had. The recipe name sounds exotic, but the “Indian” additions are flavors that nicely complement (and add the perfect zing to) the nacho base. A home run of a recipe.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Very good and different than your typical nacho.  We ate if for dinner.  My son, who is almost a vegetarian loved it.  The other, non-vegetarian son, loved it too.  

I didn’t use the full amount of cheese and felt that it was plenty. 

It took a bit longer to make than I anticipated due to the several steps, making the cilantro chutney, then the syrup with lime and the oil with cumin seeds.  It was worth the time but next time I will be forewarned.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This didn’t do it for me! Tasted kind of bland to be honest. Also I found the ghee kind of overwhelming, if you’re doubting that aspect I would say toast the cumin seeds and add them to the chutney. It definitely needs the cumin flavor but the butter mouthfeel was a bit much.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: 14 minutes til cheese melts.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Love, love, love these. Makes a great TV dinner. Indianish nachos and Netflix for the win.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made the front page of The Boston Globe food section for this similar recipe: (the NYT of Boston): http://indiannachos.com/

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is a "Meatless Monday" family favourite. Although it's not "Indian-ish," I always serve it with guacamole. I don't find the tamarind to be necessary.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
For tamarind sauce:
Heat ½ - 1 tsp ground cumin in the bottom of a dry pan until fragrant, about 30 seconds,
Add ¼ cup each tamarind paste, date syrup/maple syrup, juice of ½ lime and a pinch of salt.
Simmer over low heat until heated though.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made a simplified version of this with what we had on hand and it was awesome: chips, black beans (  adobo), onion, tomatoes, pre-made chicken tikka masala curry, and cheese. The tikka masala added such a great flavor to otherwise ordinary nachos. They were a big hit with guests.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
"Found date syrup at the Indian market. 
For tamarind sauce: Heat ½ - 1 tsp ground cumin in the bottom of a dry pan until fragrant, about 30 seconds,  Add ¼ cup each tamarind paste, date syrup/maple syrup, juice of ½ lime and a pinch of salt.  Simmer over low heat until heated though"

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Who knew we needed a recipe for Nachos? My husband almost fell over with happiness when he saw what we were having for dinner. Proportions of all toppings were perfect, flavors were great; will make again soon.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: WOW. These are hands down the best nachos I’ve had, and I’m not normally a nacho person. You can find date-tamarind paste at some international grocers. I mixed it with a bit of water to thin it out and a few generous sprinkles of ground cumin since I didn’t have ghee or cumin seeds for the chhonk. Make these! You won’t regret it.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
As other reviewers suggested, I added extra black beans and oven roasted chickpeas. It was delicious! 

Note that if you aren’t able to find tamarind sauce store bought, you can make some from paste or concentrate. Add 2T paste to 2 cups boiling water, add 1 cup jaggery sugar/brown sugar, 1/2 t ginger, cumin, salt, and cayenne. Simmer it down for ~20 minutes and you’ll have a wonderful tamarind sauce!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I made this but replaced the nacho chips with pappadums as I didn't have nacho chips but had everything else to make these. I prepared the pappadums as per package directions and then added the toppings. Excellent👌🏼

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I always kind of pretend to like nachos. These, I literally couldn’t stop eating!
A couple of things: I only found tamarind concentrate, but added 2 parts water to 1 part TC and it was just fine.
And I found the coriander/cilantro chutney to be lacking a little in depth, so i added a little garlic, a little more sugar and some olive oil, and it was great.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was sooo amazing. The tamarind sauce was the star for me, giving these such a unique flavor. I recommend doubling it (we did). We prepared it quite easily from tamarind pulp, replacing the 1/4 cup of maple syrup with water, since the pulp is already sweetened and cooking it down to a creamy consistency. I used more tomatoes and onions than called for, I also think next time I’ll replace the cilantro chutney with guacamole, the flavor of the chutney got lost with everything else going on.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Perfect as is. My daughter is coming home from college and requested this as a special meal. Best when it can all be eaten the night it's cooked. Be sure to read helpful notes for how to make the tamarind sauce (this part of recipe could use clarification edit). This is a family favorite of 2020-2021.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is delicious! I’m definitely making this again.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I halved the cheese and oil, and it came out beautifully! Serves 4 as a main course.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Love this, have made it several times as-is.  I halve this recipe and we eat it for dinner.  Next time I will have to try papadums, as another poster suggested - how genius!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I can’t stand cilantro - would this be as delicious
 if I subbed the cilantro for parsley or basil?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Dang- this was a good one! Made it for superbowl party. Salty, sweet, and spicy. I used the maple syrup with lime juice and it was just fine.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
This was a big hit.  We ate it as our main course for dinner, and everyone was perfectly happy.  Wonderful flavors, thank you, Priya.  
I felt one can of black beans was only enough for the first layer of the nachos, so I mashed up a can of garbanzo beans for the second layer, which was perfect.  Next time I think I will add cubed potatoes and replace corn chips with rice crackers, to mimic the crackers from papri chaat.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Added shredded rotisserie chicken and skipped the black beans on my half for stomach reasons. Really, really good. Like really good.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Cannot eat cilantro-is there a good substitute?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Try a mint chutney instead.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Amazing!! I doubled the black beans because I love them. Other than that, I made according to directions. So good!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We thought these were too soggy

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: It’s not every day that I step back from a dish and say, “Dear god, what have I done?” There’s A LOT going on here. I used an 11oz bag of chips. I can’t imagine getting this many toppings on fewer chips. I like the sweet/savory combination. Even as a Wisconsinite, a pound of cheese in a single dish is worrisome. It’s less daunting, once it melts. The dish loses its crunch quickly, probably because of all the toppings. That said, this would be a good shareable snack.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
More cheese. Serve chutney on the side. 
Chaat is good but needs better distribution. 

Try adding curry powder, or Bobs potatoes.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I don't like cilantro. Would parsley work here?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is whack (wack?), it’s so darned good. Doubled beans, halved cheese, tripled tomatoes (which I chopped first thing, salted, drained). Oh auntie!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Sadly, I found these a bit bland. Also, it was way too much work to make the cilantro chutney and tamarind chutney just for this.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this many times- for friends and family of various levels of pickiness, and it has always been a smash hit. I highly recommend.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We’ve been adding ground meat to make it even more filling. Today: ground pork with a little curry powder. Delicious!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Crowd-pleaser for a NYE party. Would absolutely make again

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
Very wonderful idea!  Substitute Louisiana Hot Sauce  if no fresh chili is available.
Honey or cane syrup can be substituted for maple syrup or date syrup.  
Thanks for Nutritional Information  button, NYT.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I made as directed except I subbed chickpeas for black beans (I roasted the chickpeas with 2 tbsp olive oil and sprinkled with paprika, cayenne, and ground cumin for 5 mins at 425). I only needed 3 cups of shredded cheese. 

I thought the chhonk was good but might cut down on the ghee to make this healthier, or roast the cumin seeds.

Overall, a hit with even Indian guests!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: So amazing! I added avocado and I had to make a fake tamarind that didn’t include maple, because I didn’t feel like buying a whole bottle just for this. I used some honey, a little water, a generous splash of soy sauce and some pickled jalapeño pepper juice because I didn’t feel like cutting opening another lime. It worked!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Made the nachos slightly more Indian-ish by seasoning the beans with curry powder and graham masala.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Also substituted tamarind sauce with pomegranate molasses - super tasty, ended up adding extra pomegranate molasses!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Didn’t have red onion, so I heated the tamarind sauce and tossed/simmered sliced white onion in it for a few minutes. A revelation!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These were amazing! The chutney, tamirind, chonk, and yogurt combo on top are a delight!! Thank you Krishnas!!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: delicious.....make sure your nachos are cooked crisp, or the lovely sauce, can make a few chips, softer.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Just icky, I love nachos and love Indian cuisine, but not together.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is my second time making this. I would cut back the cilantro chutney by half. The rest of this is wonderful.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Make the tamarind sauce according to the suggestions in the comments (don’t use the concentrate straight from the store). Use 2 cans of black beans and only half the red onion.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: A sheet of parchment paper on the sheet pan before putting everything down would be helpful.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Tasty but next time I would sprinkle potato filling (samosa filling) on as well. Put more cheese that I think. We didn't have ghee and did not like the oil..

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Yum! We didn't use cheese because we forgot to grab it at the store, so we made a raita to add at the end. For the chutney, I had already made some from a Maneet Chauhan recipe, so this was a good way to use it. We also added eggplant and mango, then subbed lentils for the beans. Clearly, lots of changes based on our pantry, but this was a great starting point and an awesome idea!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Unsalted butter if no Gee, extra tomato and extra beans

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Added some chaat mix on top v good and sweetened the yogurt a bit

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Wow! What a taste sensation! Looked a bit weird but decided to make it based on the reviews. It’s hard to capture how unique and exciting the flavours are in this one. Like someone else suggested, I was planning to add pickled jalapeños but forgot until I was eating. But there was already such a massive party going on in my mouth that I thought my head might explode if I added jalapeños.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: For our hungry family

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: It’s easy it’s tasty and filled with so many different flavors!!! Obsessed.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are amazing... absolutely my favorite nacho recipe!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: There’s a lot to talk about with this absolutely 5 start recipe but let’s just start with what a wildly BRILLIANT sub maple and lime is for traditional red tamarind chutney. Make this eat this.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: We made this recipe years ago and said tonight, “remember those Indian nachos” they were so good and unique they have stood out in our memories so long. Thankfully we had most of the ingredients on hand and could make it again! Even our toddler took more than a couple bites!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! So good and a fun family meal. We added a little mango chutney to use up what we had on hand and it was definitely a welcome addition

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are the best nachos I’ve ever had and will forever be disappointed with traditional nachos! Best part of watching the Super Bowl today. Went to an Indian market and found tamarind date sauce and pre-made pani puri. I made half of the pan with tortilla chips and the other half with pani puri. Both were fantastic but an extra special touch with the pani puri.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody:
I was inspired by this recipe and one of the comments about subbing tikka masala sauce cooked shredded chicken for black beans.

I wondered what else Trader Joe’s would have on hand to save me some time, food waste and be lower in sodium. 

Trader Joe’s® shopping list: b/s chicken thighs, unsalted organic tortilla chips, Zhoug Sauce*, limes and shredded Swiss Cheese & Gruyère Cheese (so much less sodium than cheddar). I also bought fresh made Pico de gallo at my local grocery. Yum!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: These are insanely good! As others suggested, I only used half of the cheese, and it was just right. Couldn’t find tamarind sauce, but was able to find tamarind concentrate, to which I added finely chopped dates, some chili powder, paprika and a little water and whizzed out together. It had a nice sweet-tart flavor that went well with the nachos & cilantro chutney.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Next time I’m going to lower the chips to sauces ratio ( fewer chips)

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: we used naan and added chicken in a store bought butter sauce. we used paneer for the cheese. delish.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: I have never used tamarind before. The only tamarind sauce in my grocery store is orange tamarind sauce. Would that taste good in this recipe?  Online I have seen Date tamarind sauce which seems like it might be a better match but it costs twice as much. I also saw many comments for making sauce from tamarind paste.  Which option(s) do you prefer in this recipe?

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: Surprised how much we loved these!  Tamarind sauce is amazing on nachos!

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I feel like the cilantro chutney and tamarind sauce could be substituted with a bottled chutney for a faster meal. But the chhonk is absolutely what made this so good.

author: Ritu Krishna
recipeTitle: Indian-ish Nachos With Cheddar, Black Beans and Chutney Recipe
commentBody: This is one of our favorite recipes. We recently started using tostadas instead of tortilla chips. Much easier to serve, and if you have a picky toddler like us, it’s super easy to set aside a tostada or two for them prior to adding the tasty sauces!

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: These were so good, I couldn’t stop my husband from finishing them off before I sent them as part of cookie boxes. Next time I would increase the cardamom to closer to 1/2 teaspoon as the cardamom flavor was barely detectable. I also used semi sweet chocolate instead of milk chocolate as I’m not a fan of milk chocolate. I highly recommend these to all chocolate peanut butter lovers.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Because I love the nutty flavor, instead of ghee I used browned butter (essentially the same as ghee, but is heated until the butter caramelizes and has an even nuttier and richer flavor). You have to let the browned butter cool a bit after making it, but so worth it for the flavor it adds to laddoos!

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Sounds yummy. Need to add the amount of coconut oil in the ingredients.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: The actual newspaper article says it is 1 tsp of coconut oil.  Hope that helps.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Since the function of ghee is to enable to use butter in cooking at higher temperatures without the milk solids separating out and burning, and the ground peanut mixture doesn't get cooked, one could simply use butter here.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: @Lee Rosenthal you can make your own ghee with unsalted butter! Only takes 10 mins or so. No other ingredients required. Plenty of good how-to’s online.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody:
Delicious!! Reminded me of some of the Indian sweets I've had in the past. Very good. The jaggery is definitely key for a lot of the flavor. I microwaved the jaggery (lol) and then threw it into the Vitamix instead of trying to make it into a powder. Worked fine. 

Had a hard time making them into balls (probably because of the melted jaggery) so I tried to freeze them to make them easier to mold but they were too firm at that point. I just covered them in the chocolate and called it a day.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody:
Eager to try this recipe but it needs a few edits.
Should it read coconut oil or ghee? Or have you left out amount of coconut oil in the ingredient list?

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Used brown sugar in place of jaggery per sub mentioned in instructions and it was far too grainy. Recipe is too finicky, also found it too runny like others mentioned. These are la-don'ts...next time will just add cardamom to regular buckeyes.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Traditionally, there is no chocolate in Indian peanut laddu. You can leave out the steps with chocolate.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Made as directed (w/brown sugar). The laddoo mixture was extremely runny once I added the ghee. I tried to compensate by adding more peanuts and brown sugar but it never turned into a peanut butter consistency. Very runny, impossible to form into a ball. This sounds like a common result. Is there a solution?

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: Add puffed rice to the mix and thank me later.

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: These are delicious. I used white chocolate instead and used 1/3 of the jaggery. Was so good!

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody:
I am a Buckeye.   The chocolate peanut butter Buckeyes have been around since at least the year of Woody Hayes 'national championship team. I thank Hetal Vasavada for improving the recipe.    The raw peanuts would add a good crunch.
